question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: nodes] Can an unprivileged external user inject multiple block node entries with the same host but different ports, exploiting the getMergeKey() logic in BlockNodeProperties to cause node deduplication failures, resulting in the importer connecting to an unintended endpoint that serves blocks with modified transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: nodes] Can an unprivileged external user set a block node host to an internal metadata service endpoint (e.g., 169.254.169.254 for AWS IMDS), causing the importer to make SSRF requests that leak cloud credentials, which the attacker then uses to access the real block stream bucket and inject blocks with modified fees?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: sourceType] Can an unprivileged external user set sourceType=FILE and simultaneously poison the cloud storage bucket (via compromised credentials obtained through SSRF) with block files containing TokenFeeScheduleUpdate transactions that set fees to values outside design parameters, causing the importer to persist fraudulent fee schedules?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: autoDiscoveryEnabled] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the autoDiscoveryEnabled=true default combined with a writable database (via SQL injection in another component) to insert malicious block node entries into the discovery cache, causing the importer to stream from an attacker-controlled node that serves blocks with fee values outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: frequency] Can an unprivileged external user set frequency to Duration.ofMillis(1) (below the @NotNull constraint but not below any @Min constraint), causing the scheduler to invoke block ingestion at maximum rate, creating a time-of-check-to-time-of-use race condition where fee schedule updates are processed out of order, resulting in incorrect fee values being persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: stream] Can an unprivileged external user set maxBlockItems to Integer.MAX_VALUE via external config, causing the importer to accept arbitrarily large blocks from a malicious block node, where the attacker has embedded thousands of fee schedule update transactions that overwhelm the fee processing pipeline and cause incorrect fee states?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: ledger] Can an unprivileged external user set ledger to null (since the @Valid annotation is present but @NotNull is absent on the ledger field), disabling TSS ledger verification entirely, allowing the importer to accept blocks from any source including those with modified fee transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: ledger] Can an unprivileged external user provide a malformed `historyProofVerificationKey` (e.g., all-zero bytes) via external config, causing the WRAPS verification to always succeed or always fail in a predictable way, allowing attacker-controlled blocks with modified fee data to pass verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: nodes] Can an unprivileged external user set a block node with port=0 (minimum allowed by @Min(0)), causing the importer to connect to a system-assigned port on the target host, potentially hitting an unintended service that returns crafted block data with modified fee transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: nodes] Can an unprivileged external user set a block node host to a DNS name they control, and use DNS rebinding to initially resolve to a legitimate IP for TLS verification, then rebind to an internal service that serves blocks with modified fee schedules, bypassing the requiresTls check?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of URL validation in getBucketName() to inject a bucket name that, when used by the cloud storage client, resolves to an attacker-controlled S3-compatible endpoint serving block files with TokenFeeScheduleUpdate transactions containing fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: sourceType] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the AUTO sourceType fallback behavior — where BLOCK_NODE is tried first, then FILE — by deliberately causing all configured block nodes to fail (e.g., via DDoS), forcing the importer to fall back to a FILE source in an attacker-controlled bucket with modified fee data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: stream] Can an unprivileged external user set maxSubscribeAttempts=1 and responseTimeout=100ms, causing legitimate block nodes to be rapidly marked inactive after a single timeout, triggering the CompositeBlockSource to switch to the FILE source where an attacker has pre-staged blocks with modified fractional fee denominators?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: nodes] Can an unprivileged external user inject a block node with an IPv6 address that bypasses IP allowlist checks (if any), connecting to an internal network service that serves blocks with fee schedule updates containing amounts outside the permitted range?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: enabled] Can an unprivileged external user set enabled=true while ledger is null (no @NotNull on ledger field), activating block stream ingestion without TSS verification, allowing any block source to inject transactions with arbitrary fee values that get persisted to the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: nodes] Can an unprivileged external user set a block node host to `localhost` or `127.0.0.1`, causing the importer to connect to a locally-running attacker process (e.g., planted via a previous exploit) that serves blocks with modified fee transactions, bypassing external network controls?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: stream] Can an unprivileged external user set maxStreamResponseSize to a value just above the size of a legitimate block but below the size of a block containing many fee schedule updates, causing the importer to silently drop fee update transactions that exceed the size limit, leaving the mirror node with stale fee data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: ledger] Can an unprivileged external user set nodeContributions to an empty list (bypassing the @NotEmpty constraint if validation is not triggered at runtime), causing the TSS verification to have no node contributions to verify against, accepting any block including those with modified fee data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network-to-bucket-name mapping in ImporterProperties.HederaNetwork.getBlockStreamBucketName() by setting the network to a custom/unknown value that maps to a predictable or attacker-controlled bucket name, causing the importer to download blocks with modified fee schedules?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: nodes] Can an unprivileged external user set a block node with priority=0 and a host that resolves to a cloud provider's internal metadata endpoint, causing the importer to leak IAM credentials via SSRF, which are then used to upload tampered block files with modified fee transactions to the legitimate bucket?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: frequency] Can an unprivileged external user set frequency to a value that causes the @Scheduled annotation's fixedDelayString to evaluate to 0 or negative, causing the block ingestion scheduler to spin continuously and create a denial-of-service condition that prevents legitimate fee updates from being processed in order?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: stream] Can an unprivileged external user set readmitDelay to Duration.ofSeconds(10) (minimum allowed), causing inactive block nodes to be rapidly readmitted and retried, creating a rapid oscillation between block sources that results in fee transactions being processed from multiple sources simultaneously, causing duplicate or conflicting fee records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: nodes] Can an unprivileged external user inject a block node with a host containing special characters (e.g., `@`, `#`, `?`) that are not validated by @NotBlank, causing the gRPC channel construction to misparse the host and connect to an unintended endpoint that serves blocks with modified fee data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: sourceType] Can an unprivileged external user set sourceType=BLOCK_NODE while nodes is empty (default), causing the importer to attempt block node connections that always fail, triggering error handling paths that may fall back to an unverified source where fee data can be modified?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: ledger] Can an unprivileged external user set historyProofVerificationKey to a valid-length but attacker-controlled byte array, causing the WRAPS signature verification to accept blocks signed by the attacker's key, allowing ingestion of blocks with fee schedule updates that set fees to values outside design parameters?
